report_name: TUANZ Calls for "Security by Design" in New Zealand's Digital Future

identities.name: Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ)
identities.description: The New Zealand tech users association that published a position paper advocating for 'security by design' and 'secure by default' principles to shift security responsibility from end-users to providers.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC)
identities.description: New Zealand's national cyber security agency, mentioned by TUANZ as a valuable institution whose efforts are not sufficient to counter the scale of modern threats alone.

identities.name: Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises (SMEs)
identities.description: A key beneficiary group of the proposed 'security by design' policy shift in New Zealand, which aims to lower their security burden.

identities.name: Individual Citizens
identities.description: A class of beneficiaries in New Zealand who would be better protected from scams, fraud, and data theft under the proposed 'security by design' framework.

identities.name: New Zealand Government
identities.description: The entity called upon by TUANZ to create the legislative and regulatory framework to enforce 'security by design' principles.

identities.name: Technology Providers and Developers
identities.description: A class of entities that would face increased responsibility and potential liability for the security of their products under TUANZ's proposed policy shift in New Zealand.

relationships.source_name: Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ)
